title: Phil Kay
description: Legendary Scottish comedian, Phil Kay, returns to Brighton Fringe for 2 nights only. His unforgettable style of live standup will leave you wondering what just happened, as his whirlwind-like humour rips through the room like a tornado, yet tenderly tickles your funny bones. Phil has been crashing onto stages since 1989 with all the energy and fervour of an illegal rave, the improvisation skills of a professional shoplifter, and the comedy timing of a showbiz giant. His crackpot stories, unpredictable asides, and maniacal stage presence set him apart from mainstream comedy circuits, earning him a core following of lifelong devotees. Come and be converted, this one is not-to-be-missed! 'An enthralling, edge-of-the-seat high wire act, when he delivers, the laughs are all the louder' (Guardian) 'It's a case of gripping the side of the seat and holding on' (The Herald) 'Fertile, feral, other-worldly and exploding with energy' (The Scotsman) 'A master of the improvised' (The Skinny)
content_warnings: Phil Kay is likely to deliver his performance with a raised voice and the use of shouting - no other loud noises are included, however
